item.title: defunding privacy in medical care
item.description:
The Drey Dossier is diligently researched and is not a conspiracy site.

The Dangerous World of Biotech Wearables, from the Drey Dossier is on youtube and explains the surveillance tech we're getting instead of healthcare. Weirdly, the federal government is rolling out these wearables to states in exchange for giving up access to hospitals, nurses,
item.content:encoded:
The Drey Dossier is diligently researched and is not a conspiracy site.

The Dangerous World of Biotech Wearables, from the Drey Dossier is on youtube and explains the surveillance tech we're getting instead of healthcare. Weirdly, the federal government is rolling out these wearables to states in exchange for giving up access to hospitals, nurses,
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: California Chemical Tank Emergency At F-35 Supplier Comes Amid Far-Left Campaign Against Defense Firms
item.description:
California Chemical Tank Emergency At F-35 Supplier Comes Amid Far-Left Campaign Against Defense Firms

By the end of last week, dozens of lawsuits had been filed against GKN Aerospace after a tank explosion risk at its Garden Grove, California, facility across Garden Grove, Anaheim, Stanton, Buena Park, Cypress, and Westminster.

Local authorities lifted the final evacuation orders last Tuesday after pressure inside the tank stabi
item.content:encoded:
California Chemical Tank Emergency At F-35 Supplier Comes Amid Far-Left Campaign Against Defense Firms

By the end of last week, dozens of lawsuits had been filed against GKN Aerospace after a tank explosion risk at its Garden Grove, California, facility across Garden Grove, Anaheim, Stanton, Buena Park, Cypress, and Westminster.

Local authorities lifted the final evacuation orders last Tuesday after pressure inside the tank stabi
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: How flat is replacing fat in AWS data center networks
item.description: How flat is replacing fat in AWS data center networks
item.dc:creator: Rafael Reyes

item.title: Fighting While Talking, Horses And Security
item.description:
Fighting While Talking, Horses And Security

By Peter Tchir of it is worth a look. Not just an Iran update, but we also cover Cuba, Russia/Ukraine, the China Summit, and Nigeria (I certainly need to get more up to speed on Africa). We will examine in the section we have decided to label Horses. While it feels like we’ve been talking about ProSec in one shape or form for well over a year (because we have), rather than gett
item.content:encoded:
Fighting While Talking, Horses And Security

By Peter Tchir of it is worth a look. Not just an Iran update, but we also cover Cuba, Russia/Ukraine, the China Summit, and Nigeria (I certainly need to get more up to speed on Africa). We will examine in the section we have decided to label Horses. While it feels like we’ve been talking about ProSec in one shape or form for well over a year (because we have), rather than gett
item.dc:creator: Rafael Reyes

item.title: Looking to see if anyone has gone to an online college that doesn’t require you to do a facial scan verification ID check.
item.description:
Looking to do an online college but the few I’ve checked all do facial scan for ID verification. Anyone have any experience with an online college that doesn’t?

submitted by
item.content:encoded:
Looking to do an online college but the few I’ve checked all do facial scan for ID verification. Anyone have any experience with an online college that doesn’t?

submitted by
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: Patient Data Privacy
item.description:
Hello, I am so grateful for this community!

I’m seeking to learn more about patient data privacy as my local medical system adopted Epic and added vague yet sweeping AI and Epic data sharing terms to their consent agreement to receive treatment.

This subreddit has already been helpful as I learn about HIE opt-outs but in case such experts or resources exist,
item.content:encoded:
Hello, I am so grateful for this community!

I’m seeking to learn more about patient data privacy as my local medical system adopted Epic and added vague yet sweeping AI and Epic data sharing terms to their consent agreement to receive treatment.

This subreddit has already been helpful as I learn about HIE opt-outs but in case such experts or resources exist,
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: The Surveillance Hubs: How Data Centers Enable The Modern Police State

item.content:encoded: submitted by   <a   <span><a href="
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: Turns Out TVs Are Monitoring Us Too
item.description:
Recent Atlantic article outlines how TVs have become so cheap. It's partly because they are tracking what we are watching and selling that data to companies.

I'll link the article in the comments. Any non smart TVs y'all have bought??

submitted by
item.content:encoded:
Recent Atlantic article outlines how TVs have become so cheap. It's partly because they are tracking what we are watching and selling that data to companies.

I'll link the article in the comments. Any non smart TVs y'all have bought??

submitted by
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: Not sure if this is an old thing, but I started to touch up my old computer from years of inactivity, and I saw this really creepy message when trying to change browsers.
item.description:
Since I can't actually put the screenshot in for some reason, I'll just say what it says.

I typed in "best browser for windows 11", and then Edge shows this popup saying

"All you need is right here" with the message being powered by Microsoft and a bunch of other crap. VERY disturbing stuff…Honestly made me glad that I'm more technologically a
item.content:encoded:
Since I can't actually put the screenshot in for some reason, I'll just say what it says.

I typed in "best browser for windows 11", and then Edge shows this popup saying

"All you need is right here" with the message being powered by Microsoft and a bunch of other crap. VERY disturbing stuff…Honestly made me glad that I'm more technologically a
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: Age Verification is only the first step
item.description:
Today I was thinking about the age verification discussion going on. Then it hit me. It's only the first step. Knowing that Meta is main driver behind all that (and probably Google/Alphabet too), it is clear that this can only be the first step.

When age verification is implemented on OS level, every app can request it (at least that is what I understood). That also
item.content:encoded:
Today I was thinking about the age verification discussion going on. Then it hit me. It's only the first step. Knowing that Meta is main driver behind all that (and probably Google/Alphabet too), it is clear that this can only be the first step.

When age verification is implemented on OS level, every app can request it (at least that is what I understood). That also
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: Qksms vs Google messages
item.description:
First off, I'm a certified newbie. Also I realize signal/molly is the best. And I use it as much as possible. But 99% of my communication is in text, and I cannot get my contacts to use signal. i got a couple to join, but I've found out they both recently uninstalled it because I was their only contact on it.

I'm wondering if my goal is to degoogle, but I value priv
item.content:encoded:
First off, I'm a certified newbie. Also I realize signal/molly is the best. And I use it as much as possible. But 99% of my communication is in text, and I cannot get my contacts to use signal. i got a couple to join, but I've found out they both recently uninstalled it because I was their only contact on it.

I'm wondering if my goal is to degoogle, but I value priv
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: Netherlands regulator fines online auction site Ticketveiling.nl €270,000 over hidden bidding bots that mislead users into believing they were competing against other people
item.description: submitted by
item.content:encoded: submitted by
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: I typed “sperm donation” in a temporary AI chat and Instagram somehow knew
item.description:
I was using a temporary chat on Grok and searched a topic through Google Keyboard (Gboard). The topic was sperm donation.

I never said it out loud. I never searched it in Chrome. I just typed it inside that temporary chat.

A short while later, Instagram started showing me sperm donation ads.

Maybe it's a coincidence. Maybe it's ad targeting working in w
item.content:encoded:
I was using a temporary chat on Grok and searched a topic through Google Keyboard (Gboard). The topic was sperm donation.

I never said it out loud. I never searched it in Chrome. I just typed it inside that temporary chat.

A short while later, Instagram started showing me sperm donation ads.

Maybe it's a coincidence. Maybe it's ad targeting working in w
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: I need an alternative to google forms to make a survey with
item.description:
Google forms will only let me use a google account. I dont have some insane threat model but I will be posting the form under this account and would rather not tie a google account back to my reddit account. Are there any free and open source google forms alternatives, and is this something I even need to worry about at all?

submitted by
item.content:encoded:
Google forms will only let me use a google account. I dont have some insane threat model but I will be posting the form under this account and would rather not tie a google account back to my reddit account. Are there any free and open source google forms alternatives, and is this something I even need to worry about at all?

submitted by
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: Shortages And Rationing Loom As Global Oil Reserves Fall At Fastest Rate In History
item.description:
Shortages And Rationing Loom As Global Oil Reserves Fall At Fastest Rate In History

What I have just laid out for you is the best case scenario.

Ultimately, what we end up facing could be so much worse.

Over the past couple of months, global oil reserves have been falling …

The SPR’s most re

item.dc:creator: Elena Vasquez

item.title: Green Retreat: California Eases Carbon-Market Costs For Oil Refiners
item.description:
Green Retreat: California Eases Carbon-Market Costs For Oil Refiners

California's green-energy regime has hollowed out the state's refining and oil industry, leaving motorists paying the highest gasoline prices in the country. AAA data show the state gasoline average now north of $6 per gallon, compared with a national average of roughly $4.36 as of Saturday morning.

The result of political blowback in California over unaffordable gasoline and diesel prices at the p
item.content:encoded:
Green Retreat: California Eases Carbon-Market Costs For Oil Refiners

California's green-energy regime has hollowed out the state's refining and oil industry, leaving motorists paying the highest gasoline prices in the country. AAA data show the state gasoline average now north of $6 per gallon, compared with a national average of roughly $4.36 as of Saturday morning.

The result of political blowback in California over unaffordable gasoline and diesel prices at the p
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: Can I get FB Marketplace to stop asking for my ID?
item.description:
The account I’ve had for a few years is now being held hostage and I can’t post any new items. For privacy reasons I didn’t use any of my real information other than my first name. And for a multitude of reasons I don’t want this public, for-profit, government lapdog corporation to have my ID.

I’m obviously not a scammer, I’ve only had 5 star reviews on a few dozen i
item.content:encoded:
The account I’ve had for a few years is now being held hostage and I can’t post any new items. For privacy reasons I didn’t use any of my real information other than my first name. And for a multitude of reasons I don’t want this public, for-profit, government lapdog corporation to have my ID.

I’m obviously not a scammer, I’ve only had 5 star reviews on a few dozen i
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: You're not resisting age verification hard enough. (US centered)
item.description:
There is still not enough opposition. I don't get why people sleep on this or even use their dislike for a company to not take action. This chicken mentality angers me a lot and people need to start thinking for once. this is not aimed at those who are actively resisting. for those that are, you're fine.

We are at a point where we got legislatures trying to shove age
item.content:encoded:
There is still not enough opposition. I don't get why people sleep on this or even use their dislike for a company to not take action. This chicken mentality angers me a lot and people need to start thinking for once. this is not aimed at those who are actively resisting. for those that are, you're fine.

We are at a point where we got legislatures trying to shove age
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: Kibbutz Children's House,The Last Piece of the Puzzle...
item.description:
Read this carefully before it gets taken down!!

Complete radio silence. No investigations, no deep-dive documentaries,absolute secrecy. For decades, the reality of this practice was buried entirely out of sight. It was only when a few individuals managed to break away from these community that the world finally caught a glimpse o
item.content:encoded:
Read this carefully before it gets taken down!!

Complete radio silence. No investigations, no deep-dive documentaries,absolute secrecy. For decades, the reality of this practice was buried entirely out of sight. It was only when a few individuals managed to break away from these community that the world finally caught a glimpse o
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: Privacy and the .NET Framework SDK?
item.description:
Anyone know if I’m at risk for data scraping from Microsoft or anyone else if I use the .NET SDK? I’ll be working on a project in Linux and considering using C#.

submitted by
item.content:encoded:
Anyone know if I’m at risk for data scraping from Microsoft or anyone else if I use the .NET SDK? I’ll be working on a project in Linux and considering using C#.

submitted by
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: privacy respecting blood pressure watch?
item.description:
Is there a watch similar to Apple's or Google's FitBit that does blood pressure and respects your privacy? Thanks.

submitted by
item.content:encoded:
Is there a watch similar to Apple's or Google's FitBit that does blood pressure and respects your privacy? Thanks.

submitted by
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: Moscow Signs Military Partnership With Taliban In Full Circle Since CIA's Operation Cyclone
item.description:
Moscow Signs Military Partnership With Taliban In Full Circle Since CIA's Operation Cyclone

reported this week.

The deal was concluded during the International Security Forum held in Moscow. According to the report by Interfax’s correspondent, Taliban Defense Minister Mullah Mohammad Yaqoob held talks with Secretary of Russia's Security Council Sergei Shoigu on the sidelines of the event.
item.content:encoded:
Moscow Signs Military Partnership With Taliban In Full Circle Since CIA's Operation Cyclone

reported this week.

The deal was concluded during the International Security Forum held in Moscow. According to the report by Interfax’s correspondent, Taliban Defense Minister Mullah Mohammad Yaqoob held talks with Secretary of Russia's Security Council Sergei Shoigu on the sidelines of the event.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Calloway

item.title: Crypto And AI Could Be Dirty Words On 2026 Midterm Campaign Trail
item.description:
Crypto And AI Could Be Dirty Words On 2026 Midterm Campaign Trail

Profligate spending during the last federal elections in the US has led to unprecedented policy changes favoring the crypto industry, with indications that a full legislative framework in the form of the CLARITY Act is on its way to becoming law.

But this hasn’t endeared the crypto industry to voters. Rece
item.content:encoded:
Crypto And AI Could Be Dirty Words On 2026 Midterm Campaign Trail

Profligate spending during the last federal elections in the US has led to unprecedented policy changes favoring the crypto industry, with indications that a full legislative framework in the form of the CLARITY Act is on its way to becoming law.

But this hasn’t endeared the crypto industry to voters. Rece
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: UK's Ofcom Investigates Airing Of Trump Interview Calling Climate Change A "Hoax"
item.description:
UK's Ofcom Investigates Airing Of Trump Interview Calling Climate Change A "Hoax"

One of the most critical components of the British censorship system is Ofcom, the Office of Communications, which regulates the broadcasting, internet, telecommunications, and postal industries.

The most recent controversy is detailed in the
item.content:encoded:
UK's Ofcom Investigates Airing Of Trump Interview Calling Climate Change A "Hoax"

One of the most critical components of the British censorship system is Ofcom, the Office of Communications, which regulates the broadcasting, internet, telecommunications, and postal industries.

The most recent controversy is detailed in the
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: China & Crypto. Understanding the evolving laws prohibiting ownership, promotion, and money making.
item.description: TL;DR - You can't just point the finger at China and say, "China's banned bitcoin for the 500th time now. You have to understand the framework the government uses for it.
item.content:encoded: TL;DR - You can't just point the finger at China and say, "China's banned bitcoin for the 500th time now. You have to understand the framework the government uses for it.
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: How do most people out there are fine with age verification everywhere?
item.description:
the time is close where all social media, apps, games, even the p*rn or might be any websites out there requires all your personal data, might be ssn, photo, or even biometrics.

right now for me with high concern of privacy, i think i will be super paranoid browsing internet and cannot sleep at all because i am sure that they already retain our data since i actively
item.content:encoded:
the time is close where all social media, apps, games, even the p*rn or might be any websites out there requires all your personal data, might be ssn, photo, or even biometrics.

right now for me with high concern of privacy, i think i will be super paranoid browsing internet and cannot sleep at all because i am sure that they already retain our data since i actively
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: 'We Outright Grabbed The Wallets': Bessent Boasts $1BN In Iran State Crypto Seized To Date
item.description:
'We Outright Grabbed The Wallets': Bessent Boasts $1BN In Iran State Crypto Seized To Date

Washington's economic war on Iran and its 'shadow' banking network continues, as on Friday Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced the US has seized $1 billion in Iranian cryptocurrency assets as part of the economic component of President Trump's Operation Epic Fury.

The billion dollar figure represents the running total seized to date, bu
item.content:encoded:
'We Outright Grabbed The Wallets': Bessent Boasts $1BN In Iran State Crypto Seized To Date

Washington's economic war on Iran and its 'shadow' banking network continues, as on Friday Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced the US has seized $1 billion in Iranian cryptocurrency assets as part of the economic component of President Trump's Operation Epic Fury.

The billion dollar figure represents the running total seized to date, bu
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: US Service Members Targeted Via Commercial Location Data, Pentagon Tells Senators
item.description:
US Service Members Targeted Via Commercial Location Data, Pentagon Tells Senators

Adversaries have used commercially-available location data to attack individual US service members in war zones, according to a report furnished by the Department of Defense to Oregon Sen. Ron Wyden, and first reported by
item.content:encoded:
US Service Members Targeted Via Commercial Location Data, Pentagon Tells Senators

Adversaries have used commercially-available location data to attack individual US service members in war zones, according to a report furnished by the Department of Defense to Oregon Sen. Ron Wyden, and first reported by

item.title: Bitcoin ETFs Bleed $2.8B In Record 9-Day Outflow Streak
item.description:
Bitcoin ETFs Bleed $2.8B In Record 9-Day Outflow Streak

Spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded another $223 million in net outflows on Thursday, marking the record nine-day outflow streak since the funds launched in 2024,

US spot Bitcoin ETF outflows in May 2026 versus February 2025. Source:
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin ETFs Bleed $2.8B In Record 9-Day Outflow Streak

Spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded another $223 million in net outflows on Thursday, marking the record nine-day outflow streak since the funds launched in 2024,

US spot Bitcoin ETF outflows in May 2026 versus February 2025. Source:
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: Papers, Please: Online Age Checks Create a Pointless Privacy Risks— One of the world’s leading age verification providers(clients include Meta, OnlyFans, Sony PlayStation, and TikTok) collect and share highly sensitive personal data—including facial photos and device fingerprints—with third parties.
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: The Secret Garden of Rock-Paper-Scissors
item.description: The Secret Garden of Rock-Paper-Scissors
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: Both Sides Agree Iran Deal 'Close' But Not Finalized, As Trump Promises 'Final Determination' Soon
item.description:
Both Sides Agree Iran Deal 'Close' But Not Finalized, As Trump Promises 'Final Determination' Soon

Summary

  • NYT: President Trump’s meeting in the Situation Room lasted about two hours, but the president did not reach a decision on any new deal with Iran.
  • Trump repeats conditions on Iran for lifting US naval blockade, oil pushed lower. Fars responds: "Mix of truth & lies."
item.content:encoded:
Both Sides Agree Iran Deal 'Close' But Not Finalized, As Trump Promises 'Final Determination' Soon

Summary

  • NYT: President Trump’s meeting in the Situation Room lasted about two hours, but the president did not reach a decision on any new deal with Iran.
  • Trump repeats conditions on Iran for lifting US naval blockade, oil pushed lower. Fars responds: "Mix of truth & lies."
item.dc:creator: Elena Vasquez

item.title: How do you think they did this?
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said at a Reagan Economic Forum event that the United States has seized $1 billion of Iranian cryptocurrency money as part of its effort to squeeze Iran’s economy. “Just outright grabbed the wallets,” Bessent said, referring to the accounts where digital currency is stored. “Some of them may be typing in right now and might not realize their
item.content:encoded: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said at a Reagan Economic Forum event that the United States has seized $1 billion of Iranian cryptocurrency money as part of its effort to squeeze Iran’s economy. “Just outright grabbed the wallets,” Bessent said, referring to the accounts where digital currency is stored. “Some of them may be typing in right now and might not realize their
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

Trump Refiles Lawsuit Over Wall Street Journal Article Linking Him To Epstein Letter

Trump’s legal team submitted the revised complaint exactly on the May 27 deadline set by U.S. District Judge Darrin Gayles. In April, Gayles Tyler Durden Fri, 05/29/2026 - 15:40

item.title: Ask HN: Question for Startup Founders on tracking emotions and cognitive signals
item.description: Hey HN, I've noticed there's been a strong aversion and tone in regard to companies tracking emotional and cognitive signals to make decisions. I'd like to get people's opinions on this and possibly understand if there's a frame where you would be FOR this in running your own startups. What is your stance on businesses tracking Emotional Intelligence? Would it make sense i
item.content:encoded: Hey HN, I've noticed there's been a strong aversion and tone in regard to companies tracking emotional and cognitive signals to make decisions. I'd like to get people's opinions on this and possibly understand if there's a frame where you would be FOR this in running your own startups. What is your stance on businesses tracking Emotional Intelligence? Would it make sense i
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: Canadian Government Is Crushing Indie Media With Two Sneaky Policies
item.description:
Canadian Government Is Crushing Indie Media With Two Sneaky Policies

Canada is not only going the way of Europe with the country's draconian speech laws, it is in many ways surpassing the suppression and censorship across the Atlantic. The speed at which the population is being robbed of their freedoms is staggering, and much of this is being done through backdoor bureaucracy.

One factor that consistently frustrated the globalist Trudeau regime during the pandemic
item.content:encoded:
Canadian Government Is Crushing Indie Media With Two Sneaky Policies

Canada is not only going the way of Europe with the country's draconian speech laws, it is in many ways surpassing the suppression and censorship across the Atlantic. The speed at which the population is being robbed of their freedoms is staggering, and much of this is being done through backdoor bureaucracy.

One factor that consistently frustrated the globalist Trudeau regime during the pandemic
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: 'Some form of crisis is almost inevitable': The $38 trillion national debt will soon be growing faster than the U.S. economy itself, watchdog warns
item.description: 'Some form of crisis is almost inevitable': The $38 trillion national debt will soon be growing faster than the U.S. economy itself, watchdog warns Fortune
item.content:encoded: 'Some form of crisis is almost inevitable': The $38 trillion national debt will soon be growing faster than the U.S. economy itself, watchdog warns Fortune
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves
